page: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated, local-disk-enabled server instance belonging to the XEIDSv4 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration delivers 80 virtual CPUs across 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity is 504.0 GB, yielding approximately 6.3 GB of RAM per virtual core. Storage is bundled with 2458 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and passmark CPU evaluations, alongside high memory bandwidth, reaching 4152.66 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 214.64 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform exceptionally well, with Redis handling 64.91 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is moderate, as reflected in average scores for single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core metrics.

A notable tradeoff is found in LLM text generation, where the lack of hardware acceleration results in poor performance, generating only 5.56 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Conversely, prompt processing speeds remain average. Given the high memory capacity, substantial local storage, and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, non-GPU workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput Redis caching layers, software compilation, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized server instance from the XEIDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 80 vCPUs (40 physical cores), 504.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers strong multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operations, as evidenced by handling 64.91 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core execution and LLM text generation are weaker, with the latter scoring in the bottom 10%. This resource density makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and enterprise applications that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated server featuring 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated x86_64 server with 80 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy databases.
tagline: An isolated, high-memory server with substantial local storage, optimized for demanding database workloads and high-throughput multi-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504 GB of RAM
2458 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage support
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Excellent throughput for database operations and Redis caching
Poor performance for LLM text generation due to lack of GPU acceleration
Ideal for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database hosting
